findings.evidence: `GaussianPolynomialIntegrable.lean:39` already proves the general statement privately; `GaussianPolynomialIntegrable.lean:70` specializes it with `a := (1 : ℝ) / 2`; Mathlib has the coefficient-general source theorem at `GaussianIntegral.lean:114`.
findings.fix: Make the positive-coefficient theorem public, e.g. `integrable_eval_mul_exp_neg_mul_sq {a : ℝ} (ha : 0 < a) (p : ℝ[X])`, and keep this standard case only as a corollary if needed.
findings.issue: `Polynomial.integrable_eval_mul_gaussian` hard-codes `Real.exp (-(x ^ 2 / 2))`, while the natural reusable theorem is for any `a : ℝ` with `0 < a`.

findings.evidence: `GaussianPolynomialIntegrable.lean:60` already proves the positive-coefficient integer-polynomial statement privately; `GaussianPolynomialIntegrable.lean:79` specializes it with `a := (1 : ℝ) / 2`; the roadmap target needs the standard case but does not require hiding the natural general form.
findings.fix: Make `integrable_aeval_mul_exp_neg_mul_sq {a : ℝ} (ha : 0 < a) (p : ℤ[X])` public and derive the roadmap's standard-Gaussian theorem from it.
findings.issue: `Polynomial.integrable_aeval_mul_gaussian` repeats the same under-generalization for integer polynomials, exporting only the `1/2` coefficient case.

summary: The added Gaussian-polynomial integrability API is exported only at the standard-normal coefficient even though the proof and Mathlib support every positive Gaussian coefficient.
